name: Search LinkedIn companies and add them to Airtable CRM
description: Automated workflow: Search LinkedIn companies and add them to Airtable CRM. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, airtable, splitOut, set. It contains 20 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## LinkedIn Company Search
This section initiates the workflow and searches for your target companies on LinkedIn using the Ghost Genius API. 

You can filter and refine your search using keywords, company size, location, industry, or even whether the company has active job postings. Use the "Set Variables" node for it.

Note that you can retrieve a maximum of 1000 companies per search (corresponding to 100 LinkedIn pages), so it's important not to exceed this number of results to avoid losing prospects.

**Example:** Let's say I want to target Growth Marketing Agencies with 11-50 employees. I do my search and see that there are 10,000 results. So I refine my search by using location to go country by country and retrieve all 10,000 results in several batches ranging from 500 to 1000 depending on the country.

**Tips:** To test the workflow or to see the number of results of your search, change the pagination parameter (Max Pages) in the "Search Companies" node. It will be displayed at the very top of the response JSON.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Company Data Processing 
This section processes each company individually.

We retrieve all the company information using Get Company Details by using the LinkedIn link obtained from the previous section.

Then we filter the company based on the number of followers, which gives us a first indication of the company's credibility (200 in this case), and whether their LinkedIn page has a website listed.

The workflow implements batch processing with a 2-second delay between requests to respect API rate limits. This methodical approach ensures reliable data collection while preventing API timeouts.

You can adjust these thresholds based on your target market - increasing the follower count for more established businesses or decreasing it for emerging markets.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## CRM Integration
This section handles the Airtable CRM integration.

It first checks if the company already exists in your database (using LinkedIn ID) to prevent duplicates because when you do close enough searches, some companies may come up several times.

If it's a new company, it adds the record to Airtable with comprehensive details including name, LinkedIn URL, website, tagline, description, and category. 

The workflow automatically tags companies as 'Growth Marketing Agency 11-50 🌍' and sets the country to '🇺🇸 United States' (customize according to your use case).

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## Introduction
Welcome to my template! Before explaining how to set it up, here's some important information:

This automation is very easy to implement and is designed for anyone wanting to build and enrich a solid CRM through LinkedIn research.

The initial data source can be changed as long as you have the LinkedIn URL of the company.

For any questions, you can send me a DM on my [LinkedIn]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} :)

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Setup
- Create an account on [Ghost Genius API]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and get your API key.

- Configure the Search Companies and Get Company Info modules by creating a "Header Auth" credential:
**Name: "Authorization"**
**Value: "Bearer api_key"**

- Create an Airtable base named "CRM" and add at least the following columns: name (default), website (url), LinkedIn (url), id (number).

- Configure your Airtable credential by following the n8n documentation.

- Add your company search selection criteria to the “Set Variables” node.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

assignments.name: B: 1-10 employees, C: 11-50 employees, D: 51-200 employees, E: 201-500 employees, F: 501-1000 employees, G: 1001-5000 employees, H: 5001-10,000 employees, I: 10,001+ employees

parameters.content:
## LinkedIn Company Search
This section initiates the workflow and searches for your target companies on LinkedIn using the Ghost Genius API. 

You can filter and refine your search using keywords, company size, location, industry, or even whether the company has active job postings. Use the "Set Variables" node for it.

Note that you can retrieve a maximum of 1000 companies per search (corresponding to 100 LinkedIn pages), so it's important not to exceed this number of results to avoid losing prospects.

**Example:** Let's say I want to target Growth Marketing Agencies with 11-50 employees. I do my search and see that there are 10,000 results. So I refine my search by using location to go country by country and retrieve all 10,000 results in several batches ranging from 500 to 1000 depending on the country.

**Tips:** To test the workflow or to see the number of results of your search, change the pagination parameter (Max Pages) in the "Search Companies" node. It will be displayed at the very top of the response JSON.

## Company Data Processing 
This section processes each company individually.

We retrieve all the company information using Get Company Details by using the LinkedIn link obtained from the previous section.

Then we filter the company based on the number of followers, which gives us a first indication of the company's credibility (200 in this case), and whether their LinkedIn page has a website listed.

The workflow implements batch processing with a 2-second delay between requests to respect API rate limits. This methodical approach ensures reliable data collection while preventing API timeouts.

You can adjust these thresholds based on your target market - increasing the follower count for more established businesses or decreasing it for emerging markets.

## CRM Integration
This section handles the Airtable CRM integration.

It first checks if the company already exists in your database (using LinkedIn ID) to prevent duplicates because when you do close enough searches, some companies may come up several times.

If it's a new company, it adds the record to Airtable with comprehensive details including name, LinkedIn URL, website, tagline, description, and category. 

The workflow automatically tags companies as 'Growth Marketing Agency 11-50 🌍' and sets the country to '🇺🇸 United States' (customize according to your use case).

## Introduction
Welcome to my template! Before explaining how to set it up, here's some important information:

This automation is very easy to implement and is designed for anyone wanting to build and enrich a solid CRM through LinkedIn research.

The initial data source can be changed as long as you have the LinkedIn URL of the company.

For any questions, you can send me a DM on my [LinkedIn]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} :)

## Setup
- Create an account on [Ghost Genius API]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and get your API key.

- Configure the Search Companies and Get Company Info modules by creating a "Header Auth" credential:
**Name: "Authorization"**
**Value: "Bearer api_key"**

- Create an Airtable base named "CRM" and add at least the following columns: name (default), website (url), LinkedIn (url), id (number).

- Configure your Airtable credential by following the n8n documentation.

- Add your company search selection criteria to the “Set Variables” node.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Process Each Company" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Company Info" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter Valid Companies" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Check If Company Exists" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Is New Company?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add Company to CRM" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search Companies" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract Company Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Variables".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
